title: [Bug] First message to picortex bot on a brand-new DM gets no reply (discovery race)
description:
Symptom (Jacob, 2026-04-23): DMing picortex@bot.globalbr.ai on chat.globalbr.ai for the first time, the bot does NOT reply. The SECOND message reliably gets a reply. Jacob suspected a timing issue.

Root cause: CONFIRMED — conversation-discovery race.

Evidence (replicated 2026-04-24 03:37-03:38 UTC):
- Fresh test user e2e_fresh_1777001825 creates NEW DM with bot via POST /api/chat/conversations (participantIds=['picortex_bot_...'], type='direct'). Convo id: bFh8_dWEIXwNvNEH3hwJF.
- Tester immediately sends 'FIRST msg' (message id qXVCMaK2THy3zKYZMPtUT). Broadcast via io.to('conversation:bFh8...').emit('message:new', ...). Bot is NOT in that room yet → not delivered.
- ~2s later picortex's 30s poll fires (03:37:47), sees the new convo (total 3→4), emits conversation:join, enters the room.
- Tester sends 'SECOND msg' at 03:38:00 (id 3uUODTcqrOLrrFa3cpdUb). Bot receives it and replies 2s later with 'Hey! Got your message. What can I help you with?'.
- picortex journalctl confirms: the first message id NEVER appears in logs; the second message id does. Bot-side socket only received the convo it just joined.

Root cause in code
- server/src/websocket/chatHandler.ts message:send handler (line 148): io.to('conversation:' + conversationId).emit('message:new', message). Only sockets that have previously called conversation:join are in the room.
- server/src/routes/chat.ts POST /conversations (lines 66-138): creates Conversation + PARTICIPATES_IN edges, returns 201. NO socket emit to notify the other participants that they are now in a new conversation.
- No conversation:created, conversation:new, or participant:added event exists on the socket server.
- Bot-side mitigation is a 30s poll (OpenChat-3ld: 'Periodic re-poll every 30s for newly-added conversations'), which creates a 0-30s window where the first message is silently dropped.

Proposed fix (server side — this ticket)
- In chat.ts POST /conversations: after CREATE, for each participant, io.to(<their user room / sockets>).emit('conversation:created', conversation) — or emit 'participant:added' to each participant.
- Options for addressing sockets per user: (a) have every socket join a 'user:${userId}' room on connect in chatHandler.ts, then emit io.to('user:' + participantId); (b) iterate userSockets map.
- Consider same event for 'add participant to existing conversation' flow (groups) once that UI ships — solves the same race for groups.
- Optionally: also buffer/retry the initial message:send if it finds zero room members who are actual participants — but the proper fix is the event.

Proposed fix (bot side — track via OpenChat-3ld)
- picortex listens for conversation:created / participant:added; on receipt, immediately GET conversation and emit conversation:join before any message arrives.
- Keep the 30s poll as a fallback.

Repro script
- /Users/Jacob/code/picortex/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8b9f846/scripts/e2e-openchat-new-convo.mjs
- Env: OPENCHAT_JWT_SECRET, TESTER_USER_ID, TESTER_EMAIL, BOT_USER_ID (default picortex_bot_7ecd2883dbd5ceef). Use a FRESH tester user that has no prior conversation with the bot (create in Neo4j first).
- Verdict field 'hypothesis_confirmed' prints true when first msg gets no reply and second msg does.

Related tickets
- OpenChat-3ld (picortex OpenChatChannel impl; v0.2 bullet listens for conversation:added — this is that event, needs server side)
- OpenChat-aoy (Group chats validation — same race affects adding bot to existing group)
- OpenChat-8bk (picortex bot user flow)
- OpenChat-qz0 (Add connection recovery for missed messages — related but not the same issue)

root_cause_confirmed: yes
close_reason: Shipped. OpenChat fd4c381 + picortex 6eea1c5. Server emits conversation:created to per-user sockets; picortex OpenChatChannel auto-joins on receipt. Verified via e2e-openchat-new-convo.mjs against prod: FIRST msg in fresh DM now gets bot reply in ~2.1s (msgId Ap24Rimu83fD1FdlHyrW0 -> reply LSkyTkwakKE_gf4f52b94). hypothesis_confirmed: false.

title: Update DESIGN_INTENT to match current contact search and port defaults
description: Design doc still states client-side contact filtering and old port defaults. Align with current behavior or update defaults.
acceptance_criteria:
- DESIGN_INTENT reflects server-side contact search
- Port defaults documented accurately
close_reason: Updated DESIGN_INTENT to reflect server-side contact search and port defaults.

title: Add Redis adapter for Socket.io multi-instance support
description:
Currently Socket.io stores connections in local memory. If we scale to multiple backend instances, users on Server A can't chat with users on Server B.

**Fix:** Implement @socket.io/redis-adapter

From Gemini audit: 'Socket.io scaling'

title: Expose conversation participants in conversation list response
description: Conversation list entries lack participant data, causing direct chats to show as Unknown until a separate fetch. Update API to include participants in GET /api/chat/conversations and ensure POST /api/chat/conversations returns participants too.
acceptance_criteria:
- GET /api/chat/conversations returns participants array for each conversation
- POST /api/chat/conversations returns participants array
- Direct chat titles render correctly from list response
close_reason: Conversation list/create now include participants in API responses.

title: Secure SSO token exchange (don't pass JWT in URL)
description:
SECURITY: Passing JWTs in URL query parameters is a security risk.

**Current:** GET /auth/callback?token=jwt_here
URLs are logged in browser history, proxy logs, server access logs.

**Fix options:**
1. Use fragment identifier (#token=...) - not sent to server
2. Use short-lived exchange code (OAuth2 pattern) - POST to exchange for JWT
3. Use HttpOnly cookies for session management

From Gemini audit: 'Passing JWTs in URL query parameters is a security risk'
close_reason: SSO callback now prefers auth code/fragment token; JWTs are no longer required in URL query.

title: Publish iOS mobile app via WebView wrapper on TestFlight
[Bot user] picortex OpenChatChannel: socket.io-client + auto-join + message:send/new
description:
Step 2 of picortex-8bk. In ~/code/picortex/src/channels/openchat.ts: replace NotImplementedError stub with a real socket.io-based channel that:
- Connects on boot with bot JWT
- On connect: GET /api/chat/conversations → emit conversation:join for each
- Listener: message:new, filter self-sent, dispatch to picortex.dispatcher.onInbound
- send(): emits message:send with callback
- Periodic re-poll every 30s for newly-added conversations (v0.2: listen for conversation:added event)
- Register in server.ts alongside LinqChannel; gated on OPENCHAT_BOT_JWT presence

title: Emit typing indicators from message input
description: Typing indicators are wired in socket but never emitted by the client. Add start/stop emissions with debounce to avoid spam.
acceptance_criteria:
- Typing start emitted while user types
- Typing stop emitted after inactivity or send
- No excessive socket spam
close_reason: Message input now emits typing start/stop with debounce.

title: Fix contact search to use server-side API (scalability critical)
description:
CRITICAL: The frontend loads ALL contacts and filters locally. This will crash browsers at scale.

**Current behavior:**
- loadContacts() fetches all users
- Frontend filters with contacts.filter()

**Required fix:**
- Remove default 'load all' behavior
- Call API with search term: GET /api/chat/contacts?q=searchTerm
- Debounce search input (300ms)
- Show 'Type to search...' prompt instead of loading everyone

From Gemini audit: 'If Noos has 10,000 users... the Frontend loadContacts() puts all 10k objects into React State'
close_reason: Fixed in commit bb3ecd8. Frontend now uses server-side search with debouncing.

title: [Bot user] Create picortex :User node in prod Neo4j; provision long-lived JWT; document
description:
Step 1 of the bot-user wiring sprint (picortex-8bk). 
- Create :User{id, email:'picortex@bot.globalbr.ai', name:'picortex', presenceStatus:'available', isBot:true} in prod Neo4j
- Generate JWT with openchat JWT_SECRET, 1-year expiry, payload {userId, email}
- Document both in /opt/picortex/app/.env (OPENCHAT_* env vars)
- This ticket = the DB + secret work. Code work = picortex's OpenChatChannel impl (separate ticket in picortex repo).

title: Add system-adaptive dark mode support
description:
Implement dark mode that follows system preferences.

Features:
- Detect prefers-color-scheme media query
- Toggle between light/dark based on system setting
- Optional manual override in settings
- Apply to all components via Tailwind dark: variants

Reference: TailwindCSS dark mode docs

**Note:** Also implement for Thoughtstreams (~/code/Thoughtstreams)

title: Add UI for presence status and status message
description: Presence status update is supported server-side but there is no UI to set it. Add a simple control in the sidebar for status and message.
acceptance_criteria:
- User can set presence status
- User can set a status message
- Updates propagate via existing socket/api
close_reason: Sidebar now includes presence status and status message controls.

title: [Group chats] Validate picortex bot works in group conversations end-to-end
description:
Sibling of picortex-hal (P2, picortex-side group-chat validation pass). Today picortex is only tested/used in DMs (type: 'direct') on chat.globalbr.ai. The bot auto-joins any conversation it's a participant in via polling GET /api/chat/conversations, but OpenChat may have different routing semantics for group vs direct conversations and there is no 'add bot to group' flow.

Scope
- Verify end-to-end: bot added as participant in a type='group' Conversation, message:new fires for bot on group messages, bot's message:send via socket broadcasts correctly to all group participants.
- Audit server/src/routes/chat.ts and server/src/websocket/chatHandler.ts for any branch that treats type='group' differently from type='direct' (permission checks, room membership, deduplication, etc.).
- Test scenarios: (a) bot in group from creation, (b) bot added to existing group after creation, (c) bot removed from group, (d) multiple humans + bot in same group, (e) bot-only messages vs human-to-human messages (discriminate/mention-only behavior).

Likely implementation gaps
- UI: no 'add bot to group' flow in the OpenChat web client (see OpenChat-88z new-conversation contact picker for nearby UX).
- Server: no participant:added socket event emitted when a user is added to an existing conversation — bot would miss it until next 30s poll (same race as the first-message bug, see sibling ticket 'First message to picortex gets no reply').
- Server: no discrimination between 'bot should reply to every message in DM' vs 'bot should only reply when mentioned/addressed in group'. Add per-conversation bot-behavior flags (quiet mode, mention-only).

Dependencies
- Blocked-by / related-to: sibling bug ticket 'First message to picortex gets no reply' — same discovery race affects newly-added participants in groups.
- Related: picortex-hal (picortex-side validation pass, P2, filed earlier today)
- Related: OpenChat-3ld (picortex OpenChatChannel impl) — v0.2 bullet already lists 'listen for conversation:added event'; that event should also cover participant:added for groups.
- Related: OpenChat-8bk (picortex bot user flow)

Acceptance
- Green e2e: human creates group with bot, sends message, bot receives and replies. Same again after bot is added mid-conversation.
- Documented bot-behavior defaults for groups (mention-only by default is reasonable).
- Any server changes land behind feature flag until picortex-hal ships the matching client-side support.

title: Implement SSO redirect for Thoughtstreams login
description:
Mirror the SSO redirect flow from OpenChat-es7 for Thoughtstreams.

When implementing OpenChat SSO redirect, ensure the same pattern works for Thoughtstreams:
1. User visits Thoughtstreams without auth → redirect to Noos login
2. Noos authenticates → redirects back to Thoughtstreams with token
3. Thoughtstreams exchanges token for session

This should share the same Noos SSO infrastructure.

**Dependency:** Implement after OpenChat-es7 (use it as template)
**Affects:** Thoughtstreams repo (~/code/Thoughtstreams)
close_reason: Thoughtstreams SSO implemented in Thoughtstreams-kwu - using same pattern as OpenChat

title: Implement SSO redirect flow for OpenChat login
description:
The OpenChat login page should redirect to Noos for authentication instead of having its own login form.

**Current state**: Uses /api/auth/dev-login for development, placeholder /api/auth/login endpoint exists

**Target flow**:
1. User visits OpenChat without auth → redirect to Noos login page
2. After Noos login → redirect back to OpenChat with token (via callback URL)
3. If already logged in to Noos → seamless redirect with token exchange
4. Handle ?redirect_uri param to return user to the original OpenChat page they were trying to access

**Implementation**:
- Standard OAuth/SSO redirect pattern
- OpenChat frontend: detect no token → redirect to Noos with return URL
- Noos: add OpenChat to SSO_APPS config
- OpenChat: /auth/callback route to receive and store token
- Consider using existing Noos SSO infrastructure
close_reason: Login now redirects to Noos SSO authorize with state/redirect; callback exchanges code for session.

title: Sync SSO flow with Thoughtstreams / latest Noos pattern
description:
OpenChat SSO diverges from Thoughtstreams:

1. Thoughtstreams proxies /api/auth/sso-exchange through its own server; OpenChat calls Noos directly from the browser (cross-origin, requires CORS, leaks Noos URL, bakes in VITE_NOOS_URL at build time). Refactor OpenChat to POST to /api/auth/sso-exchange on its own server and have the server talk to Noos internally via NOOS_API_URL (already in docker-compose). Benefits: no cross-origin fetch, no VITE_NOOS_URL dependency for the exchange path, matches Thoughtstreams.

2. Thoughtstreams calls /auth/session-check on load to auto-resume a Noos session. OpenChat does not — users must click Continue with Noos even if already signed in on ts.globalbr.ai. Add session-check for true cross-app SSO.

3. Consider extracting a shared noos-sso npm module (used by OpenChat, Thoughtstreams, thoughtstream-gemini-jacob) so auth drift stops happening per-app. Tracked under noos-sso-ecosystem epic.

title: Add token login option (paste Noos JWT) to login UI
description: Design intent calls for token login. Add a login mode that accepts a JWT and uses existing ChatContext.login() to store it.
acceptance_criteria:
- Login page includes a token login mode
- Pasted JWT is stored and grants access to chat
- Errors are shown for invalid tokens
close_reason: Added token login mode to login UI with validation feedback.

title: SSO "Failed to fetch" — client bundle baked with localhost NOOS_URL
description: Production chat.globalbr.ai shows "SSO Login Failed / Failed to fetch" after successful Noos login. Root cause: client/dist bundle calls http://localhost:52743/api/auth/sso-exchange — the build was not run with VITE_NOOS_URL=https://globalbr.ai. In client/src/api.ts and App.tsx the default fallback is http://localhost:52743. Fix: add client/.env.production with VITE_NOOS_URL=https://globalbr.ai so the env is committed, then rebuild and redeploy. Also change the hardcoded fallback in source from http://localhost:52743 to https://globalbr.ai (or throw) so forgetting the env var no longer breaks prod. Also deploy.sh has a stale SERVER_IP=44.211.180.200 — current lightsail IP is 3.216.129.34.
close_reason: Deployed to chat.globalbr.ai; verified Alice SSO login succeeds end-to-end. Bundle no longer contains localhost:52743. Commit 6d61f20.

title: Add dev-login support for AI agent testing
close_reason: Already implemented - POST /api/auth/dev-login exists in server/src/routes/auth.ts

title: Add connection recovery for missed messages
description:
When user loses internet and reconnects, they may miss messages.

**Fix:** Implement socket.on('reconnect', ...) handler that fetches latest messages to ensure data consistency.

From Gemini audit: 'Connection Recovery'

title: Jacob Test quick-login returns Invalid password on Noos
description: On https://globalbr.ai/auth/authorize the "Jacob Test" quick-login button (fills tmad4000@gmail.com) returns "Invalid password". Alice and Bob work. Either the test-account seed password drifted or tmad4000@gmail.com is the real Jacob account (not a test account) and should be relabeled. Noos-side fix, not OpenChat.

title: Real-time sidebar updates and unread message indicators
description:
**Problem:**
Two related issues with message notifications and real-time updates:

1. **Sidebar doesn't update for non-active conversations**
   - When you have conversation A open and receive a message in conversation B, the sidebar doesn't show that B has new messages
   - No unread badge/indicator appears
   - Conversation order doesn't update to show most recent at top
   - Have to click on the conversation to see there's a new message

2. **New contact messages require page refresh**
   - When someone new messages you, it doesn't appear in the conversation list
   - Must refresh the entire page to see the new conversation

**What's idiomatic in chat apps (Slack, Discord, WhatsApp, Messenger):**

1. **Unread indicators:**
   - Red dot/badge with unread count on conversations
   - Bold text for conversation name when unread
   - Preview text shows latest message

2. **Conversation list behavior:**
   - Conversations reorder in real-time (most recent at top)
   - New conversations appear automatically at top
   - Visual highlight/animation when new message arrives

3. **Real-time updates via WebSocket:**
   - Already have WebSocket for messages in active chat
   - Need to also push updates to sidebar state
   - When message received for conversation X: update X's lastMessage, lastMessageAt, unreadCount

**Implementation approach:**

1. **State management:**
   - Track unreadCount per conversation
   - Update when: message received for non-active conversation
   - Clear when: user opens that conversation

2. **WebSocket events:**
   - On new_message event: update the affected conversation in sidebar state
   - On new_conversation event: add to conversation list

3. **UI components:**
   - Add unread badge component to ConversationListItem
   - Sort conversations by lastMessageAt (descending)
   - Highlight/animate on new message

**Affects:** Core chat UX - users expect real-time updates in any modern chat app
